description: Hooks add predictability/guarantees to the-loop's PDLC process. The SessionStart hook runs session-start.sh, which (1) auto-upgrades the installed plugin so every new session runs the latest the-loop — fast-forwarding the plugin's git checkout to origin, best-effort and non-blocking; opt out with THE_LOOP_AUTO_UPGRADE=0 (issue #38) — and (2) surfaces the project's the-loop config so the harness loads its rules. This file is Claude Code hook format; in Cursor the same reminder ships as the always-applied rule rules/the-loop.mdc (Cursor has no SessionStart equivalent, and it resolves the plugin from the repo directly, so it self-upgrades without a hook). Quality gates (lint/typecheck/test) are documented for projects to wire to their own tooling, since commands are project-specific.
